subject: Statistics
title: Number Needed to Treat
description: A 60-second lesson on Number Needed to Treat.

hook.text: How many people must take a drug for one person to benefit? The answer is often shockingly high.

buildup.text: NNT is the inverse of absolute risk reduction. If the drug reduces risk by 1%, NNT = 100.

discovery.text: Statins for heart disease: NNT = 50. That means 49 out of 50 people take the pill with no benefit.

twist.text: For the one person it saves, the drug is a miracle. For the 49, it was unnecessary. Both facts are true.

climax.text: NNT forces honest thinking about who benefits. It's the most patient-friendly number in medicine.

punchline.text: Treat 100 to save 1. Is that worth it? NNT forces the question.

quiz.question: What does a Number Needed to Treat of 50 mean?
options: 50 people must take the drug for 1 person to benefit from it
The drug works for 50% of patients
50 patients were in the clinical trial
